summary: High‑energy China Cat > Rider run and tight second‑set jams
blurb: The second set rockets with a seamless China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider → Estimated Prophet → Eyes of the World sequence, followed by a powerful Gimme Some Lovin' → The Wheel transition. Jerry’s vocals and guitar are on fire, while Brent adds bright synth textures. The first set delivers solid openers and tight rhythm work.
review: June 26, 1987 at Alpine Valley captured one of the band’s most electrifying late‑summer outings. After a crisp opener with "Feel Like A Stranger" and "Franklin's Tower," the Dead built momentum into the second set, launching into an unforgettable China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider medley that highlighted Jerry’s iconic “Northbound Train” vocal line and Brent’s soaring Kurzweil synth colors. The groove only intensified through Estimated Prophet and Eyes of the World, spilling into a tight Drum/Space excursion that birthed a thunderous Gimme Some Lovin' → The Wheel transition and a blistering All Along The Watchtower. The night closed with an explosive Around & Around → Sugar Magnolia finale and a sing‑along Touch of Grey encore. While the audience recording has occasional glitches, its energy and the band’s locked‑in performance make it the definitive listening source for this show.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: One recording is clearly positive, praising the second‑set flow and individual performances; the other offers mixed feedback but still highlights strong moments like the Stranger jam. The overall sentiment leans positive with no consistent negative criticism, justifying a moderate +1.0 adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Audience tape with good overall clarity, captures the high‑energy second set and key transitions despite minor glitches; consistently praised as the most complete source.

key_highlights: China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider → Estimated Prophet → Eyes of the World run
Gimme Some Lovin' → The Wheel transition shines with tight band interplay
Explosive Around & Around → Sugar Magnolia finale
Jerry’s vocal “Northbound Train” line and tight guitar work throughout

band_performance.Jerry: Vocal “Northbound Train” line during China Cat/Rider is a standout, with tight, enthusiastic guitar work and a string‑of‑notes solo in the Stranger jam.
band_performance.Bob: Provides solid rhythm on Row Jimmy and contributes strong vocal harmonies throughout the set.
band_performance.Brent: Adds bright Kurzweil synth textures on China Cat and a tasteful “Gloria” tease, keeping the keyboard palette lively.
band_performance.Billy: Drumming is tight and in sync, especially evident during the Drum/Space sections and the high‑energy second set.
